seoTitle: South Maclean vs South Nanango — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Maclean and South Nanango. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Maclean vs South Nanango: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Maclean and South Nanango.
heading: South Maclean vs South Nanango — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Maclean, Queensland and South Nanango, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Maclean has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to South Nanango (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Maclean or South Nanango safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Maclean has a flood risk score of 25/100, while South Nanango scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Maclean scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. South Nanango scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Maclean or South Nanango?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Maclean has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
